entries.direction_title: Admissions and intake-equity regulation (keep structure, fix the gate)
entries.rationale: T1 directly regulates the admissions mechanism (exam coachability, SES-weighted proxies, algorithm inputs) while explicitly preserving the 6/8-year structure, matching S1's definition of keeping structure and fixing the gate.

entries.direction_title: Supply-side contraction of the selective track (shrink the gate, not the rules)
entries.rationale: T2 formalizes score thresholds and capacity caps to shrink the state-sector selective track's footprint while leaving admission criteria within remaining places largely unchanged, matching S2's supply-side contraction logic.

entries.direction_title: Structural delay of the selection age (Finnish/Polish model)
entries.rationale: T3 proposes raising the statutory tracking age to 14+ via legislative amendment, converting 6/8-year entry points into a unified comprehensive lower-secondary phase bundled with anti-sorting catchment rules, directly matching the Finnish/Polish structural-delay model of S3.

entries.direction_title: Non-structural compensatory reform (leave selection age unchanged)
entries.rationale: T4 leaves the 10-12 selection age and track structure entirely untouched and instead pursues targeted funding, curriculum clarity, teacher-quality levelling, and workload reduction, matching S4's Portugal-style non-structural compensatory approach.

T5 is the explicit no-new-policy counterfactual in which declining birth cohorts and existing statutory class-size minimums force passive network consolidation and shrinkage of the selective track's absolute size and share, serving as the baseline against which T1-T4 are measured.
